_comment_purpose: Foreman default configuration template. The installer wizard reads this as a starting point, then writes the user's choices into ~/.foreman/config.json. Do not edit this file by hand on a deployed shop; edit ~/.foreman/config.json instead.
_comment_curation: Channels are curated to email/slack/cli/websocket per CLAUDE.md. Other channels (discord/telegram/feishu/qq/weixin/wecom/dingtalk/whatsapp/matrix) are not surfaced in the wizard but remain in the codebase. Per-channel default is enabled:false; we omit them here so the config stays minimal.

email._comment: Set enabled:true after running the wizard's email setup step. The owner provides IMAP/SMTP creds, allow_from list, and attachment limits.

slack._comment: Set enabled:true after the wizard provisions a Slack app and writes the bot token + signing secret to the per-deployment .env file.

websocket._comment: Powers the WebUI. Always on for the local owner browser session; not exposed to the internet by default.

tools._comment_ssrf: Per-deployment policy override populated from policies/{managed-cloud,single-tenant-cloud,on-prem}.yaml at install time. Default is empty, which under nanobot's default-deny SSRF means the agent can only reach public LLM provider hostnames + the email server.
